title: Spark DSL
summary: A first-principles, usage-focused tour of Elixir's Spark — entities, sections, extensions, schemas, the compile-time pipeline, and introspection — building to why introspectable DSLs are a natural fit for LLM-agentic systems (Ash AI, Jido) and a from-scratch agent DSL an LLM can read and call.

concepts.title: The Big Idea: What Spark Is, and Why You'd Want One

images.prompt: A serene, modern editorial illustration in flat vector style: a craftsman's workbench at twilight where tiny glowing orange sparks rise and lock together into a tidy, glowing purple lattice of nested boxes — small boxes inside a medium box inside a large dashed frame — suggesting a language assembling itself. Cool lavender-and-violet palette with warm ember-orange highlights, generous whitespace, soft depth, no text baked in.
images.alt: A wizard's workbench where small glowing sparks assemble into a structured language
images.caption: Spark: many small sparks, assembled into a language.

images.prompt: A clean, modern editorial tech illustration, flat vector with soft glow: on the left, a tidy purple 'spec sheet' of nested declarative blocks; from it, three small labeled cards (each a 'tool' with a name and tiny argument rows) float rightward along glowing rails into a luminous orange AI orb that is reaching back to pick one card. Cool violet-and-lavender palette with warm ember-orange highlights, generous whitespace, no real text — just suggestive shapes. Sense of a human-written declaration being read and used by a machine mind.
images.alt: A glowing declarative spec on the left feeding a luminous AI core on the right via labeled tool cards
images.caption: Human declares the tools; the model reads the catalog and calls one.
